# Xiaomi MiMo provider — live verification evidence
# Captured: 2026-06-19  (claude_toolkit, branch main, commits up to Task 3)
# Generated by Task 3 of the Xiaomi provider plan. NO raw API key values appear below —
# only structural proof and redacted excerpts.

## Goal
Prove the `xiaomi` provider alias is correctly installed and that the transport it
configures (native Anthropic endpoint) genuinely works with the user's real key for
agentic/coding use (tool calling). Everything below was run against the REAL host state
and the REAL Xiaomi API; the key was read from ~/api_keys.sh at runtime and is never
persisted by the toolkit.

## 1. claude-providers sync — xiaomi activated
Command: `claude-providers sync`  (online, verification enabled)
Relevant line:
    provider 'xiaomi' -> alias 'xiaomi' [native] model=mimo-v2.5-pro (unverified)
    sync done: 18 active, 0 disabled (failed verify), 61 not-resolved

NOTE on (unverified): the in-process LLMsVerifier/HTTP-probe step sources ~/api_keys.sh
under `set -u`, which hits a PRE-EXISTING unrelated error in that file
("ApiKey_Google_Vertex_AI: unbound variable", line 52 — a different provider's key). This
makes the verifier fall back to "unverified" for EVERY provider, not just xiaomi. It is
NOT a failure of xiaomi and NOT caused by this change. The authoritative live proof is
the direct native-endpoint round trip in section 4 below (HTTP 200 + correct text).

## 2. claude-providers show xiaomi  —  installed env record
    CMA_PROVIDER_ID='xiaomi'
    CMA_PROVIDER_KEYVAR='XIAOMI_MIMO_API_KEY'
    CMA_PROVIDER_TRANSPORT='native'
    CMA_PROVIDER_BASE_URL='https://api.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic'
    CMA_PROVIDER_MODEL='mimo-v2.5-pro'
    CMA_PROVIDER_FAST_MODEL='mimo-v2-flash'
    CMA_PROVIDER_CONFIG_DIR='/Users/milosvasic/.claude-prov-xiaomi'

## 3. alias + config dir wired
    alias xiaomi="cma_run_provider xiaomi"          # in aliases.sh
    ~/.claude-prov-xiaomi/  (dir, created)
    ~/.claude-prov-xiaomi/plugins -> ~/.claude-shared/plugins   (symlink OK)

## 4. NATIVE-ENDPOINT ROUND TRIP (the transport the alias uses) — STRONG model
POST https://api.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic/v1/messages
  Authorization: Bearer <XIAOMI_MIMO_API_KEY>   anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
  body: {"model":"mimo-v2.5-pro","max_tokens":64,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply with exactly: XIAOMI_ALIAS_OK"}]}
Response (HTTP 200):
  {"id":"d87ddb946c0f44fe85db044a04d5c8b8","type":"message","role":"assistant",
   "model":"mimo-v2.5-pro","stop_reason":"max_tokens",
   "content":[{"type":"text","text":"XIAOMI_ALIAS_OK"},
              {"type":"thinking","thinking":"…","signature":""}],
   "usage":{"input_tokens":69,"output_tokens":64,"cache_read_input_tokens":192}}
Assertions: HTTP 200 = PASS, text "XIAOMI_ALIAS_OK" present = PASS, native Anthropic
shape (type:"message") = PASS.

## 5. NATIVE-ENDPOINT ROUND TRIP — FAST model
POST …/anthropic/v1/messages  model=mimo-v2-flash  "Say OK"
Response (HTTP 200):
  {"id":"adb1266698964712944681a103bf8a11","type":"message","role":"assistant",
   "model":"mimo-v2-flash","stop_reason":"max_tokens",
   "content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"…","signature":""}],
   "usage":{"input_tokens":57,"output_tokens":32,"cache_read_input_tokens":192}}
Assertion: HTTP 200 = PASS.

## 6. TOOL CALLING proven (OpenAI-compat path, earlier in research) — STRONG model
POST https://api.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions  model=mimo-v2.5-pro  with tools[]
Response (HTTP 200):
  finish_reason:"tool_calls", valid tool_calls[] (calc({"expr":"7*6"})),
  reasoning_content present.  => agentic/coding-capable through ccr if ever needed.

## 7. Wrapper native-branch env (faithful trace of cma_run_provider, lib.sh native path)
Sourcing xiaomi.env + ~/api_keys.sh and replicating the native-branch exports:
    ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://api.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic
    ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=sk-ssj…<REDACTED, len=51, NON-EMPTY>
    ANTHROPIC_MODEL=mimo-v2.5-pro
    ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL=mimo-v2-flash
    CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=/Users/milosvasic/.claude-prov-xiaomi
    (transport=native)
These are the exact env vars Claude Code reads in native mode. Combined with §4-5
(POSTing that base+token -> HTTP 200), the alias is proven functional.

## 8. Account-detection invariant preserved
    cma_detect_accounts -> 0 provider dirs (prov-*) detected as accounts.
    prov-xiaomi specifically: excluded. PASS.

## 9. Live provider verifier — verify_providers_live.sh  (5/5 PASS)
    - every provider env file has the required non-secret fields        PASS
    - NO secret values in env files (only CMA_PROVIDER_* lines)         PASS
    - each provider alias resolves to cma_run_provider                  PASS
    - the cma_run_provider wrapper is defined                           PASS
    - provider config dirs excluded from account detection              PASS

## 10. Sandbox code-test suite — ALL GREEN
    bash scripts/tests/run-all.sh -> 8/8 test files passed, 0 failed.
    providers test: 69 assertions (was 60; +9 for xiaomi resolver+sync coverage).

## Honest note — the only failures in the repo's run-proof.sh
run-proof.sh reports 2 failures, BOTH in the opencode live verifier
("opencode resolves the generated config :: no skill paths" and
"opencode discovers the Claude plugin skills :: skills resolved"). These are
PRE-EXISTING and ENVIRONMENTAL: opencode's `debug skill` stream returns no skills on
this host. They are unrelated to Xiaomi — verified by: (a) zero opencode files changed
by this work (git diff --stat 02c3d90..HEAD shows only providers/*.json +
tests/test_providers.sh), and (b) the opencode verifier fails identically when run
standalone. The provider verification (the relevant suite) is 5/5 PASS.
